warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.sourceTitle: Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) Online Database
rules.notes: Verification of permitted food additives and their conditions of use is required via the Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) Online Database, which is regularly updated.
rules.evidenceQuote: The "Codex General Standard for Food Additives" (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods

rules.parameter: Contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants (e.g., mycotoxins, metals like arsenic, lead, mercury)
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants thematic area / General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed
rules.notes: Verification of maximum levels (MLs) or guideline levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants is required via the Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and related Codex texts.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.limit: Verification required via Union list of food additives in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008
rules.sourceTitle: Database - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: The database serves as a tool to inform about approved food additives and their conditions of use in the EU.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria, toxins and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.limit: Verification required via Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: This regulation lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites in specific foods, and process hygiene criteria.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 - HALAL FOOD - Part 1 : General Requirements
rules.notes: This technical regulation outlines the general requirements for Halal food, which may include ingredient sourcing, processing, and labelling.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 - Labeling of prepackaged food stuffs
rules.notes: This technical regulation details the requirements for labeling of prepackaged food.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.parameter: Mandatory expiration dates for food products
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-1:2013 - Expiration dates for food products - Part 1 : Mandatory expiration dates
rules.notes: This technical regulation specifies mandatory expiration dates for food products.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية

rules.parameter: Voluntary expiration dates for food products
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-2:2013 - Expiration dates for food products - Part 2 : Voluntary expiration dates
rules.notes: This standard provides guidelines for voluntary expiration dates for food products.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-2:2013 مواصفة قياسية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الثاني : فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 1016:2015 - Microbiological Criteria for Foodstuffs
rules.notes: Specific microbiological limits for foodstuffs and food ingredients are detailed in the full regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: This GSO technical regulation is concerned with microbiological limits for some foodstuffs intended for human consumption and for some food ingredients used in food industry.

rules.sourceTitle: SE Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 tentang Ketentuan Produksi, Importasi, Registrasi, dan Peredaran Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)
rules.notes: This regulation sets out provisions for the production, import, registration, and circulation of Dinitrogen Monoxide (N2O) as a food additive. Specific limits and conditions must be verified in the full document.
rules.evidenceQuote: Ketentuan Produksi, Importasi, Registrasi, dan Peredaran Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)

rules.sourceTitle: PERBPOM Nomor 6 Tahun 2026 tentang Tata Cara Penerbitan Surat Keterangan Ekspor Pangan Olahan
rules.notes: This regulation outlines the procedures for issuing export certificates for processed foods. Compliance with relevant food safety and quality standards, which may include labelling, is typically a prerequisite for such certificates. The full document must be consulted for detailed procedures.
rules.evidenceQuote: Tata Cara Penerbitan Surat Keterangan Ekspor Pangan Olahan

rules.sourceTitle: PERKABPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019 Tentang Kategori Pangan
rules.notes: This regulation defines food categories, which is essential for determining the applicability of other specific regulations regarding additives, contaminants, and labelling for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products. The full document must be consulted for detailed categories.
rules.evidenceQuote: Kategori Pangan

rules.sourceTitle: PERBPOM Nomor 8 Tahun 2020 tentang Pengawasan Obat dan Makanan Yang Diedarkan Secara Daring
rules.notes: This regulation covers the oversight of drugs and food distributed online. It likely includes provisions for ensuring that information provided for online sales of processed foods, including labelling details, is accurate and compliant. The full document must be consulted for specific requirements.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pengawasan Obat dan Makanan Yang Diedarkan Secara Daring

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Can only be directly added to fillings and toppings in kuehs.
rules.evidenceQuote: Class II chemical preservatives can only be directly added, within the prescribed regulatory limits, to fillings and toppings in kuehs.

rules.parameter: Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
rules.limit: Not to be directly added during manufacturing
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: Class II chemical preservatives, such as sorbic acid and benzoic acid, should not be directly added when manufacturing the kueh.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: General requirement for all food sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: General requirement for all food products.
rules.evidenceQuote: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Businesses must check the 'List of Permitted Food Additives' or use the 'Food Additives Search tool'.
rules.evidenceQuote: Download the List of Permitted Food Additives or use the Food Additives Search tool to check if your additives are allowed.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Applies to all food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.

rules.parameter: Two or more Class II chemical preservatives
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Use the 'Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives' to determine permitted levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food (e.g. sorbic acid or benzoic acid), use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels that should be used.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Businesses must check that contaminants are within specified limits using relevant SFA documents.
rules.evidenceQuote: If laboratory test results indicate that there are contaminants in your products, they must be within the specified limits.

rules.limit: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: General requirement for all food sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Applies to all food businesses.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents i.e. contaminants in food.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.foodCategory: Meat and food derived from meat
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat containing these residues.
rules.evidenceQuote: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of the following compounds: diethylstibestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hexene) hexoestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-n-hexane) dienoestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-2, 4-hexadiene)

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refer to SFA's 'Heavy metals' document.
rules.evidenceQuote: Heavy metals Heavy metals

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refer to SFA's 'Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins' document.
rules.evidenceQuote: Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refer to SFA's 'Other incidental constituents' document.
rules.evidenceQuote: Other incidental constituents Other incidental constituents

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refer to Singapore's regulatory limits or Codex MRLs if no Singapore limits are specified.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pesticide residues Singapore's regulatory limits Codex MRLs (if there are no regulatory limits specified in the Food Regulations)

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refer to SFA's 'Veterinary drug residues' document.
rules.evidenceQuote: Veterinary drug residues Veterinary drug residues

rules.parameter: Veterinary drug residues (other than prohibited compounds)
rules.limit: Must comply with Maximum Residue Limits (MRLs)
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: MRLs can be downloaded from the SFA website.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.limit: Must be declared on food labels
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Applies to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refer to SFA's 'Microbiological standards Ready-to-eat food' document.
rules.evidenceQuote: Microbiological standards Ready-to-eat food

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refer to SFA's 'Non-ready-to-eat food (till 3 Mar 2024)' document.
rules.evidenceQuote: Non-ready-to-eat food (till 3 Mar 2024)

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.sourceTitle: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
rules.notes: Specific use restrictions and labeling requirements must be verified in the regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient

rules.parameter: Cephalopods and cephalopod products, Spiral shells and Spiral shell products thereof, Seeds and seed products (excluding highly refined sunflower seed oil), Kiwis and kiwi products thereof
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Labeling is recommended for these allergens.
rules.evidenceQuote: Besides the mandatory labeling allergens, food allergen labeling is also recommended for the products that contain any of the following allergens as an ingredient or food additive

rules.parameter: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish, sulphite
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: These 11 allergens require mandatory labeling if present as an ingredient or food additive. Sulphite requires labeling if residue sulphur dioxide is 10 mg/kg or more.
rules.evidenceQuote: For prepackaged foods, food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive, regulated under Point 2 of the Regulation of Food Allergen Labeling: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish, and sulphite as the residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food.

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods
rules.notes: Specific limits are detailed in the referenced standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products
rules.notes: Specific limits are detailed in the referenced standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health and Welfare - Laws and Regulations
rules.notes: Specific limits and detailed regulations for various food aspects (additive, contaminant, etc.) must be verified in the official databases.
rules.evidenceQuote: 衛生福利法規檢索系統, 全國法規資料庫

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: A specific statement is suggested if there's a risk of cross-contamination, such as 'The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○'.
rules.evidenceQuote: If there is a risk of a food product being affected by allergen cross-contamination... the following statement of precautionary allergen labeling is suggested: “ The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or the synonymous terms.

rules.sourceTitle: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products
rules.notes: Specific requirements for nutrition claims must be verified in the regulation.

rules.notes: Specific limits and permitted uses for food additives must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Specific standards for contaminants in food must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารปนเปื้อน

rules.limit: Verification required against the ASEAN MRLs list.

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Specific standards for toxic substances and veterinary drug residues must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารพิษ-ยาสัตว์ตกค้าง

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: General requirements for food and nutrition labelling must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: การแสดงฉลากอาหารและฉลากโภชนาการ

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: A list of foods prohibited from production, import, or sale exists and must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: อาหารที่ห้ามผลิต นำเข้า หรือจำหน่าย

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Specific microbiological standards for pathogenic microorganisms must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารด้านจุลินทรีย์ที่ทำให้เกิดโรค

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Delisted color additives are in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30).
rules.evidenceQuote: Delisted color additives in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30)

rules.sourceTitle: Food Additive Listings | FDA
rules.notes: Food additives and color additives are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively).
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively)

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: GRAS substances are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184) or the inventory of GRAS Notices.
rules.evidenceQuote: Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184).

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Substances approved for specific uses in foods prior to September 6, 1958, are listed in 21 CFR Part 181.
rules.evidenceQuote: Substances approved for specific uses in foods prior to September 6, 1958, known as prior-sanctioned substances (21 CFR Part 181).

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Prohibited substances are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189).
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food

rules.limit: Milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: These are the nine major food allergens recognized by U.S. law (FALCPA and FASTER Act).
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

rules.foodCategory: Processed foods (Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces)
rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is a Guidance Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is an Action Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb

rules.foodCategory: Snack foods (Peanuts and peanut products, Pistachio nuts)
rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is an Action Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Peanuts and peanut products Action Level 20 ppb

rules.parameter: Various chemical contaminants (tolerances, action levels, guidance levels)
rules.limit: Verification required against the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool and referenced guidance documents/regulations.
rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509. The tool provides a consolidated list.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list of these levels. Contaminant levels listed in the transparency tool include tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels.

rules.limit: Not required by law; should only be used to address unavoidable cross-contact after good manufacturing practices.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Advisory statements should not be a substitute for adhering to current good manufacturing practices and must be truthful and not misleading.
rules.evidenceQuote: Consumers may also see advisory statements such as “may contain [allergen] or “produced in a facility that also uses [allergen].” Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility and have taken every precaution to avoid cross-contact

rules.limit: Facilities must put written procedures in place to control allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate allergen labeling.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Established by the "Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food" rule (CGMP & PC rule, 21 CFR part 117).
rules.evidenceQuote: The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens. For example, the FDA requires facilities to put written procedures in place to control allergen cross-contact between products that contain allergens and those that are not supposed to contain them and to ensure that the products are accurately labeled with respect to allergens.

rules.limit: Must be declared on the label.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Identified as allergenic substances.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine, which are derived from insects, have been identified as allergenic substances that must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.limit: Must be specifically labeled with the name of the allergen source.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The name of the food source must appear in parentheses following the ingredient name OR immediately after/next to the ingredient list in a 'contains' statement.
rules.evidenceQuote: FALCPA requires that foods or ingredients that contain a "major food allergen" be specifically labeled with the name of the allergen source.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Required due to potential hypersensitivity reactions.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels so consumers who are sensitive to the dye can avoid it.

rules.limit: Must meet a defined standard for gluten content.
rules.effectiveDate: Final rule defining "gluten-free" issued Aug 2, 2013; compliance for fermented/hydrolyzed foods Aug 12, 2020.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling.
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling, which helps consumers, especially those living with celiac disease, be confident that items labeled “gluten-free” meet a defined standard for gluten content.

rules.parameter: Specificity for Tree Nuts, Fish, and Crustacean Shellfish
rules.limit: The type of tree nut, and the species of fish and Crustacean shellfish must be declared.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Examples: almonds, pecans, walnuts for tree nuts; bass, flounder, cod for fish; crab, lobster, shrimp for Crustacean shellfish.
rules.evidenceQuote: The law also requires that the type of tree nut (for example, almonds, pecans, walnuts), and the species of fish (for example, bass, flounder, cod) and Crustacean shellfish (for example, crab, lobster, shrimp) to be declared.

rules.limit: Must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration is ≥10 total sulfites
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Due to adverse reactions in sensitive individuals (e.g., asthma).
rules.evidenceQuote: Various sulfiting agents, including sodium bisulfite, are allowed as food ingredients. But due in part to adverse reactions to them, such as asthma in sensitive individuals, they must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.
